info.title: thewiki API
info.description: REST endpoints for thewiki — see https://github.com/i-doll/thewiki

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/admin/blocklist/ip` — add a new IP blocklist row.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Row identifier

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/admin/blocklist/url` — add a new URL blocklist row.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Row identifier

parameters.name: actor
parameters.description: Filter by actor username.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Filter by stable action code, e.g. `page.create`.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Include entries at or after this RFC 3339 timestamp.

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Include entries at or before this RFC 3339 timestamp.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Clamped by storage defaults.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous response.

parameters.name: actor
parameters.description: Filter by actor username.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Filter by stable action code, e.g. `page.create`.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Include entries at or after this RFC 3339 timestamp.

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Include entries at or before this RFC 3339 timestamp.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Clamped by storage defaults.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous response.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/auth/login` — verify credentials and issue a session cookie.
post.description:
Constant-time guarantee on the failure path: when the username is unknown,
we still run an Argon2 verify against a pre-computed PHC string. Wall-clock
time per attempt is therefore dominated by the configured Argon2 cost
regardless of which arm fires.

Rate limiting is applied by the router middleware before this handler runs.

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Optional existing session and CSRF cookies: `thewiki_session=...; thewiki_csrf=...`. Required only when a session cookie is present.

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the `thewiki_csrf` cookie. Required only when a session cookie is present.

403.description: CSRF token missing or invalid when a session cookie is present

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/auth/logout` — revoke the session and clear both cookies.
post.description:
Requires a valid session. The CSRF layer also requires the matching
`X-CSRF-Token` header because logout is a state-mutating call.

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Session and CSRF cookies: `thewiki_session=...; thewiki_csrf=...`

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the `thewiki_csrf` cookie

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: `thewiki_session` cookie

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/auth/policy` — publish the operator-configured auth shape
so the SPA can render the right affordances.
get.description:
Always available (no auth required) — by design, since the answer is what
the UI needs *before* the user has a session.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/auth/register` — create a new user account (#41 wiring).
post.description:
Honours `auth.registration`:

| Policy   | Behaviour                                                 |
|----------|-----------------------------------------------------------|
| `open`   | Anyone can register (subject to CAPTCHA if configured).   |
| `invite` | Returns 403 — invite codes ship with #38.                 |
| `closed` | Returns 403; admins create accounts manually.             |

When `captcha.apply_to_registration = true`, the handler runs
`captcha.verify` against `captcha_response` before creating the user.
A failed verification short-circuits with 400 (`captcha_failed`); a
network error with the upstream surfaces as 502 (`captcha_upstream`)
so the SPA can offer a retry.

The created user holds no roles by default — operators promote them
out-of-band. The endpoint does **not** issue a session; the SPA is
expected to redirect to `/login` after a successful 201.

403.description: Registration is closed or requires an invite

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/captcha/config` — return the provider's frontend config or
`null` when no widget should be rendered.
get.description:
The response is `application/json`; the body is either a
[`CaptchaFrontendConfig`] object or the JSON literal `null`. The SPA
branches on the latter to skip mounting the widget.

200.description: Frontend CAPTCHA config (object) or `null` when no widget should be rendered.

post.description:
Requires an authenticated session. Anonymous callers (even with
`anonymous_edits = true`) cannot mint categories — the taxonomy is an
operator concern.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/categories/{slug}` — fetch a category and its member pages.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Category slug

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. `0`/missing falls back to the route-level default.

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/media` — accept a multipart upload, dedup by content hash,
store the blob in the configured backend.
post.description:
Returns the existing `MediaView` (with status `200 OK`) when a row with
the same hash already exists; otherwise inserts a row and returns `200`.
We deliberately use `200` for both paths so the client doesn't have to
branch on "did the server actually persist this?" — the `id` and `url`
are stable either way.

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Session and CSRF cookies.

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token.

requestBody.description: Single `file` field carrying the upload bytes.

200.description: Upload accepted (or deduped against an existing row)

415.description: Content type not in the configured allowlist

get.description:
Without `?size=`, serves the original verbatim. With a valid
`?size=`, serves the matching thumbnail variant (WebP). When the
requested variant doesn't exist (vector / animated source, or
generation failed) the original is returned as a graceful fallback.

Sets `Content-Type` to the stored MIME type, `Vary: Accept` (since
future content-negotiation can rotate the response without leaking a
stale cache) and an immutable `Cache-Control` since media is
content-addressed and will never change for the same id.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the media row

parameters.name: size
parameters.description:
Variant label: `small` (≤320 px), `medium` (≤768 px), `large`
(≤1280 px). Anything else returns 400 with the list of accepted
values.

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/v1/media/{id}` — remove the metadata row and the blob.
delete.description:
Auth-gated through [`RequireAuth`] — anonymous callers always 401, even
if `auth.anonymous_edits = true`. A finer role check
(`Permissions::DELETE_MEDIA`) is a TODO once #14's role wiring lands.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: UUIDv7 of the media row

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Session and CSRF cookies.

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/namespaces` — list every namespace defined on this wiki.
get.description:
Open to anonymous callers; the SPA's page list and search results already
need this information to render namespace prefixes.

post.description:
Requires [`Permissions::MANAGE_NAMESPACES`]. Returns `201 Created` with
the new row on success, `409` if the slug is already in use.

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Session and CSRF cookies: `thewiki_session=...; thewiki_csrf=...`

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the `thewiki_csrf` cookie

delete.description:
Refuses (409) when any pages still live in the namespace; the admin must
move them first. The schema's FK enforces this on the storage side too,
but we surface the 409 with a clear message rather than letting the
database error bubble up generic.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Slug of the namespace to delete

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Session and CSRF cookies: `thewiki_session=...; thewiki_csrf=...`

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the `thewiki_csrf` cookie

patch.summary:
`PATCH /api/v1/namespaces/{slug}` — rename the display name of a
namespace.
patch.description:
Slug renames are intentionally out of scope (URL breakage); only the
human-readable label can be changed here.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Slug of the namespace to update

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Session and CSRF cookies: `thewiki_session=...; thewiki_csrf=...`

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the `thewiki_csrf` cookie

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Page size. Clamped to
[`thewiki_storage::repo::MAX_PAGE_SIZE`].

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Notification id

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description:
Namespace slug to list pages from. Defaults to `Main` if absent.
Namespace prefix routing lands with #28.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Opaque cursor returned by a previous call. Omit to start from the
beginning.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Page size. Clamped to
[`thewiki_storage::repo::MAX_PAGE_SIZE`]. `0`/missing falls back to
the route-level default.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/pages` — create a page plus its initial revision.
post.description:
Steps:
1. Resolve the namespace by slug (404 if missing).
2. Insert the page row with `current_revision_id = NULL`.
3. Insert the initial revision, authored by the caller.
4. Update the page row to point at the new revision (unless approval is
   required, in which case the page stays headless until a moderator
   promotes the pending revision).

The schema's `pages.current_revision_id` FK is `ON DELETE SET NULL`, so
the brief NULL state in step 2 is legitimate even with FK enforcement on.

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Optional session and CSRF cookies: `thewiki_session=...; thewiki_csrf=...`. Required only for authenticated edits; anonymous edits may omit them when enabled.

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the `thewiki_csrf` cookie. Required only when a session cookie is present.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/pages/{slug}` — fetch a page by slug in the default namespace.
get.description:
Read is open by design — anonymous reads are always allowed (the
`anonymous_edits` flag only gates mutating endpoints).

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: URL slug within the default namespace

put.description:
Title is optional (keeps the existing title when omitted); content always
produces a new revision row. When the approval-queue gate matches, the
revision lands in the (stubbed) queue and the response is `202 Accepted`
with the page row still pointing at the previous head.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: URL slug within the default namespace

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Optional session and CSRF cookies: `thewiki_session=...; thewiki_csrf=...`. Required only for authenticated edits; anonymous edits may omit them when enabled.

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the `thewiki_csrf` cookie. Required only when a session cookie is present.

delete.summary: `DELETE /api/v1/pages/{slug}` — remove a page and all its revisions.
delete.description:
The `revisions` table has `ON DELETE CASCADE` on `page_id`, so wiping the
page row collapses the history. Deletion is gated by the same configurable
extractor as edits — operators who flip `anonymous_edits = true` accept
that anonymous callers can delete as well. The eventual role-gated
extractor (TODO(#14): `RequireRole(Role::Admin)`) will tighten this.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: URL slug within the default namespace

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Optional session and CSRF cookies: `thewiki_session=...; thewiki_csrf=...`. Required only for authenticated edits; anonymous edits may omit them when enabled.

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the `thewiki_csrf` cookie. Required only when a session cookie is present.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/pages/{slug}/backlinks` — list the pages that link to
`{slug}` via a `[[WikiLink]]`.
get.description:
Source of truth is the `page_links` table populated on every live page
create / update (#30). Reads are open like the other page reads; the
response surface mirrors [`PageListResponse`] for paging consistency.

Returns an empty list when the target has no inbound links. We do **not**
404 on missing targets — a redlink with backlinks is a legitimate state
(the editor can create the page knowing who references it).

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: URL slug within the default namespace

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Opaque cursor returned by a previous call. Omit to start from the
beginning.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Page size. Clamped to
[`thewiki_storage::repo::MAX_PAGE_SIZE`]. `0`/missing falls back to
the route-level default.

get.description:
Both `from` and `to` must belong to the page identified by `{slug}`; a
mismatch (or an unknown revision id) yields 404 — we do not want to leak
the existence of revisions on other pages.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: URL slug within the default namespace

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Revision the diff is computed *from* — shown as `-` lines.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Revision the diff is computed *to* — shown as `+` lines.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: URL slug within the default namespace

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Session and CSRF cookies: `thewiki_session=...; thewiki_csrf=...`

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the `thewiki_csrf` cookie

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/pages/{slug}/revert` — revert a page to a historical revision.
post.description:
Steps:
1. Resolve the page by slug in the default namespace (404 if missing).
2. Load the historical revision by id (404 if missing).
3. Verify the revision belongs to this page; if not, **also 404**. We
   deliberately do not surface a 403 here — leaking the existence of
   revision ids belonging to other pages would be a cross-page oracle.
4. Commit a new revision with the historical body. Its `parent_id` is the
   page's *current* head (not the revision being reverted to) so the
   history graph stays linear and the audit trail captures both endpoints.
5. Advance `page.current_revision_id` to the new revision.
6. Write a persistent audit-log row for operators.

Returns the now-current [`PageView`].

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: URL slug within the default namespace

parameters.name: cookie
parameters.description: Session and CSRF cookies: `thewiki_session=...; thewiki_csrf=...`

parameters.name: x-csrf-token
parameters.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the `thewiki_csrf` cookie

get.description:
Returns 404 if the page itself does not exist. A page that exists but has
no revisions yet (a transient state — see the doc on
`pages.current_revision_id`) returns an empty list.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: URL slug within the default namespace

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Opaque cursor returned by a previous call. Omit to start from the
newest revision.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Page size. Clamped to
[`thewiki_storage::repo::MAX_PAGE_SIZE`]. `0`/missing falls back to
the route-level default.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description:
Restrict to one lifecycle status — `pending`, `approved`, or
`rejected`. Defaults to `pending` so the reviewer queue stays
uncluttered.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Page size. Clamped to
[`thewiki_storage::repo::MAX_PAGE_SIZE`].

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Pending revision id

post.summary:
`POST /api/v1/pending-revisions/{id}/approve` — promote to a real
revision against the target page.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Pending revision id

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Pending revision id

get.description:
Cursor-paginated, newest first. Filterable by `since`, `namespace`, and
`actor`. Unknown namespaces / actors render as `404`; an unset filter
simply drops the corresponding predicate.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
RFC 3339 timestamp. Only revisions committed at or after this point
are returned. Omit to include all history.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Namespace slug to filter on. Omit to include every namespace.

parameters.name: actor
parameters.description:
Username to filter on. Only revisions authored by this user are
returned.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Opaque cursor returned by a previous call. Omit to start from the
newest entry.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Page size. Clamped to
[`thewiki_storage::repo::MAX_PAGE_SIZE`]. `0`/missing falls back to
the route-level default.

get.description:
Axum's `matchit`-based router rejects parameters that share a path
segment with literal text (`{namespace}.atom` would error), so the
namespace feed lives under a `/atom` sub-segment. The wiki-wide feed at
`/recent-changes.atom` is unaffected because its full path is a literal.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Namespace slug

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/search` — ranked full-text search across every indexed page.
get.description:
Returns `400 invalid_input` when `q` is missing or whitespace-only.
Otherwise responds with a hit list ordered by Tantivy BM25 score
(with the configured title-boost applied to title-field matches).
Snippets carry HTML highlights wrapped in `<mark>…</mark>`; clients must
sanitise before rendering.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
Free-text query. Empty / whitespace-only values are rejected with
`400 invalid_input`.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description:
Optional namespace filter (slug). Unknown namespaces simply return
no hits — we deliberately do not 404 here because the index may
lag the canonical namespace list.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description:
Optional tag filter. Currently matches against the empty multi-valued
`tags` field, so always returns nothing until #29 lands.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Page size. `0` / missing falls back to the route default (10).
Clamped to the route maximum (50).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Opaque cursor returned by a previous call. Reserved for a follow-up
— accepted today but ignored, so clients can pass it through
without breaking when pagination lands.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description:
Optional prefix to match. An empty / missing prefix lists every
tag, clamped by `limit`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Max number of tags to return. `0`/missing falls back to the
route-level default.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Tag value (case-insensitive)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by a previous call.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size.

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/watchlist` — return every page the caller watches, newest
subscription first.

post.summary: `POST /api/v1/watchlist` — add a page to the caller's watchlist.
post.description:
Idempotent: re-watching a page already on the watchlist returns the same
`201 { watched: true }` body without bumping the original timestamp, and
without writing a second `watchlist.add` audit row.

get.summary: `GET /api/v1/watchlist.atom` — Atom feed of the caller's watched pages.
get.description:
Each entry corresponds to a watched page's latest revision so feed
readers behave the same way they do on the wiki-wide route: a fresh
`<updated>` whenever someone edits a page the user watches.

delete.summary:
`DELETE /api/v1/watchlist/{page_id}` — remove a page from the caller's
watchlist.
delete.description:
Idempotent — removing a page the user wasn't watching returns
`204 No Content` just the same. Returns `404` only when the page itself
doesn't exist (so callers can distinguish "typo'd ID" from "already
removed").

parameters.name: page_id
parameters.description: Page UUID to remove

get.summary:
`GET /api/v1/wiki/{namespace}/{slug}/talk` — fetch the talk page for the
subject page identified by `(namespace, slug)` (#43).
get.description:
Resolution rules:

- The path's `namespace` must resolve to a non-talk namespace that is
  paired with a `Talk_*` companion. The handler returns `400` if the
  path points at an unpaired namespace and `404` if the namespace
  doesn't exist.
- The talk page lives at `(paired_namespace_id, slug)`. If no such row
  exists yet, the handler returns `404` — the SPA's "Discuss" button
  uses this to switch between "Create the talk page" and "Open the
  existing discussion".

Reads are open by design, exactly like the regular page reads.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Subject namespace slug

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: Page slug within the subject namespace

400.description: Namespace has no paired talk side

404.description: Subject namespace, subject page, or talk page not found

page_id.description: Identifier of the page to add to the watchlist.

AuditLogEntryView.description: JSON representation of one audit-log entry.

target_label.description: Human target label at event time.

next_cursor.description: Cursor for the next page, if any.

AuthErrorBody.description: Wire form returned by auth endpoints on failure.

AuthPolicyPayload.description:
Wire shape of `GET /api/v1/auth/policy`.

The SPA reads this on boot to decide whether to render the "Sign up" CTA
(open / invite) and whether to surface an "Edit anonymously" affordance.
Kept narrow on purpose — operators tune fifteen knobs in `thewiki.toml`,
but only these two affect what the SPA shows the user *before* they have
a session.

anonymous_edits.description: Whether anonymous (logged-out) callers can submit edits.

approval_required_for.description:
Whether edits land in a moderator approval queue. The exact scope
(`"none"`, `"anonymous"`, `"new-users"`, `"all"`) is exposed so the
SPA can show a "your edit will be reviewed" hint before the user
clicks save.

BacklinkItem.description:
A single inbound link surfaced by
`GET /api/v1/pages/{slug}/backlinks` (#30).

namespace_slug.description: Namespace slug the source page lives in.

page_id.description: Stable identifier of the source page.

page_slug.description: URL slug of the source page.

title.description: Human-readable title of the source page.

items.description: Pages that link to the queried target, ordered `(source_page_id ASC)`.

next_cursor.description: Token to fetch the next page, or `None` if the listing is exhausted.

CaptchaFrontendConfig.description:
Public configuration the SPA needs to render a CAPTCHA challenge.

Surfaced through `GET /api/v1/captcha/config`. `provider` is a stable
short identifier (`"hcaptcha"`, `"turnstile"`, …) the SPA branches on to
pick the right widget; `site_key` is the public key the widget receives.

Sensitive material (the secret) is never included.

provider.description: Short stable identifier the SPA matches on (e.g. `"hcaptcha"`).

site_key.description: Public site key. Safe to embed in HTML / JS — never the secret.

items.description: Member pages in this category, paginated.
Every defined category, ordered by slug.

CategoryMemberView.description: A page entry surfaced by the "members of this category / tag" lists.

namespace_slug.description: Namespace slug the page lives in.

page_id.description: Stable identifier of the member page.

CategoryView.description: A category, flattened for the wire form.

slug.description: URL slug, unique across all categories.

oneOf.description:
Optional parent category id. Setting `None` makes this a top-level
category. Mutually checked against the storage layer's cycle check.

slug.description: URL slug. Must be unique across all categories.

cidr.description:
CIDR in canonical form. The handler parses with `ipnet::IpNet::from_str`
before persisting — invalid input returns 400 without touching the DB.

reason.description: Optional reason. Omitting it stores an empty string.

slug.description:
URL slug. Must satisfy
[`thewiki_core::NamespaceSlug`](thewiki_core::NamespaceSlug)'s
validation rules: ASCII alphanumerics, `_`, `-` (no `:`).

categories.description:
Optional category ids to assign on create. Omit / empty for no
categorisation.

content.description:
Initial body for the page. The first revision is committed with this
content.

namespace_slug.description:
Slug of the namespace this page lives in. Optional — the namespace
can also be carried in the URL path (`/api/v1/wiki/{namespace}`) or
defaulted to `Main` on the legacy `/api/v1/pages` route. The
namespace must already exist; the API does not create namespaces on
demand.

slug.description: URL-safe slug, unique within the resolved namespace.

tags.description:
Optional flat tag list. Strings are validated and lowercased on the
server; rejected with `400` if any tag fails validation.

title.description: Human-readable title shown in the UI.

pattern.description:
Rust `regex` pattern. Compiled with `regex::Regex::new` for validation
before persisting.

DiffHunk.description:
A unified-diff hunk: a contiguous span of changed lines plus a small
window of context on either side.

`old_start` / `new_start` are 1-based line numbers, matching the
`@@ -old_start,old_count +new_start,new_count @@` convention used by
`git diff`. When `*_count == 0` the corresponding `*_start` is `0` (this
is what `git diff` does for pure additions or deletions).

lines.description: Lines in the hunk, in display order.

new_count.description: Number of lines from the `to` side covered by this hunk.

new_start.description:
1-based line number of the first line in the `to` body, or `0` when
the hunk represents a pure deletion.

old_count.description: Number of lines from the `from` side covered by this hunk.

old_start.description:
1-based line number of the first line in the `from` body, or `0`
when the hunk represents a pure insertion.

DiffKind.description:
Kind of line in a [`DiffHunk`].

`#[non_exhaustive]` so we can add new variants (e.g. `ChangeInPlace`,
`NoNewlineAtEof`) without breaking downstream matches.

content.description:
Content of the line. Includes the trailing newline as it appears in
the source so callers can reassemble a faithful unified diff.

kind.description: Whether the line is context / insertion / deletion.

from.description: Revision the diff was computed from.

hunks.description:
Same diff, broken into structured hunks for clients that want to
render their own side-by-side view.

to.description: Revision the diff was computed to.

unified.description:
Ready-to-display unified-diff text. The `---`/`+++` header lines name
the two revisions by id for traceability.

ErrorBody.description:
The wire form of an API error.

Kept intentionally small: a stable machine-readable `code` plus a free-form
human message. New variants can be added by extending [`ApiError`] without
changing this shape.

message.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong.

IpBlocklistView.description: IP blocklist row as returned by the API.

password.description: Password in clear. Compared in constant time via Argon2id verify.

MediaView.description:
Public projection of a [`Media`] row.

`content_hash_hex` is the lowercase hex form of the SHA-256 digest — the
raw bytes would round-trip as a base64 string under `serde`, which is
less useful to clients that want to copy/paste the hash. `url` is the
canonical fetch path for the bytes.

content_hash_hex.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 digest of the stored bytes.

content_type.description: IANA media type the upload was stored as.

original_filename.description: Filename the client supplied at upload time.

NamespaceListResponse.description:
Response from `GET /api/v1/namespaces`.

No pagination — operators rarely define more than a handful of
namespaces, so a single batch is plenty.

items.description: Every namespace defined on this wiki, in creation order.

NamespaceView.description: A single namespace, returned by both the list and create endpoints.

is_talk.description:
Whether this is a discussion ("talk") namespace paired with a
subject namespace (#43).

slug.description: URL-safe slug. Forms the `<namespace>` segment of `/wiki/<namespace>/<slug>`.

NotificationId.description: Identifier for an in-app notification row (#40).

items.description: Rows in this batch, newest first.

next_cursor.description:
Token to fetch the next page, or `None` when the listing is
exhausted.

unread.description: Count of unread rows in the current user's inbox.

payload.description:
Arbitrary structured payload, identical to what the producer
attached.

read_at.description:
`None` while unread; the RFC3339 timestamp once the user opened
the row.

PageLinks.description:
Hypermedia-style link block surfaced alongside a [`PageView`] (#43).

Today the only field is `talk`, which the SPA uses to render the
"Discuss" sidebar link. Adding new links here is additive — existing
clients that ignore the `_links` block are unaffected.

talk.description:
Navigable SPA-route URL of the discussion ("talk") page paired
with this page, in the form `/wiki/<talk_ns>/<slug>`. The server
is the source of truth so the client never has to reconstruct
`Talk_<ns>` itself — that convention can drift after a rename.
`None` for pages already in a talk namespace (no "talk of a
talk") or when the namespace has no paired partner.

PageListItem.description:
Lighter representation of a page used inside [`PageListResponse`].

Lacks `content` and `namespace_id`; clients listing a namespace already
know the namespace.

namespace_slug.description: Slug of the namespace this page lives in.

updated_at.description: When the page row was last touched.

PageListResponse.description:
Response from `GET /api/v1/pages?cursor=…&limit=…`.

`next_cursor` is `None` once the listing has been exhausted; otherwise
pass it back as `?cursor=…` to fetch the next page.

items.description:
Rows in this batch, ordered `(created_at ASC, id ASC)` per the
storage layer's contract.

next_cursor.description: Token to fetch the next page, or `None` if there are no more pages.

PageView.description:
A single page returned by the read endpoints.

`content` is the body of the current revision (joined in for
convenience); `content_html` is the same body rendered through
[`thewiki_render::MarkdownRenderer`] with all `[[WikiLink]]`s resolved
against the page repository (so missing targets are styled as redlinks)
and the result sanitised by ammonia. The HTML is safe to embed.
`content_html` was added in #30 — before that the API returned only the
raw body and the SPA rendered it client-side.

Listing endpoints use the lighter [`PageListItem`] instead so they don't
have to ship every page's full body.

_links.description:
Hypermedia-style cross-references for this page. New surface added
in #43 — additive, so existing clients that ignore it are unaffected.

categories.description: Categories this page is assigned to (#29).

content.description: Body of the current revision, or empty string if no revision exists.

content_html.description:
Rendered, sanitised HTML of [`Self::content`].

Empty when `content` is empty. Wikilinks are resolved against the
page repository: missing targets render with `class="redlink"` and
a URL pointing at the create form (`/wiki/.../edit?new=1`).

created_at.description: When the page row was first created.

oneOf.description:
Pointer to the current head revision. `None` only in the transient
state between page creation and the first revision being committed
(today that window is closed inside `POST /api/v1/pages`).

is_talk.description:
`true` if this page lives in a discussion ("talk") namespace (#43).
The SPA uses this to switch the body renderer to
`<TalkThread>` and to hide the "Discuss" sidebar link (talk pages
don't have their own talk page).

namespace_slug.description:
Slug of the namespace; joined in so clients don't need a second
round trip just to render a breadcrumb.

oneOf.description:
Identifier of the `pending_revisions` row, present only when
[`Self::queued`] is `true`. Reviewers act on this id.

protection_level.description:
How protected this page is from edits. Drives the SPA's lock badge
and is enforced server-side on every mutating handler (#34).

queue_position.description:
Position of the queued edit in the FIFO pending queue at the moment
the row was written (1 = next to be reviewed). Present only when
[`Self::queued`] is `true`.

queued.description:
Set to `true` on a `202 Accepted` response when the submitted edit
landed in the moderation queue (#40) instead of going live. The page
fields above still describe the **previous** state (or, on a fresh
create, the empty placeholder page that's now reserving the slug).

signature_convention.description:
Documentation of the signature-expansion convention (#43). Surfaced
on every read response so the SPA can render a `~~~~` preview
client-side. Independent of the page's namespace — the marker is
only *expanded* on save when the page lives in a talk namespace.

slug.description: URL slug, unique within the namespace.

tags.description: Flat tag set this page carries (#29). Always lowercased.

updated_at.description: When the page row was last touched.

head_body.description:
Body of the page's *current* head revision at fetch time. Differs
from `parent_body` when another edit landed on the page between the
proposal and the reviewer opening it. `None` if the page still has
no head.

head_moved_since_proposal.description:
`true` if `head_body` differs from the proposal's parent (a
concurrent edit landed since this row was queued). The reviewer UI
uses this to render a "head has moved on" warning so approving
can't silently clobber intermediate work.

parent_body.description:
Body of the parent revision the edit was based on, joined in so the
SPA can render a diff against it. `None` when the page didn't have
a head at queue time (the row proposes the initial revision).

PendingRevisionDetailResponse.description:
Detail shape returned by `GET /api/v1/pending-revisions/{id}`.

Carries the full proposed body so the reviewer's diff view can render
without a second round trip.

PendingRevisionId.description:
Identifier for a row in the `pending_revisions` (edit approval queue)
table (#40).

items.description: Rows in this batch, newest first.

next_cursor.description:
Token to fetch the next page, or `None` when the listing is
exhausted.

total.description:
Total count of pending rows (matches the filter when `status` is
provided, otherwise counts everything). Useful for the reviewer
UI's "N pending" badge.

PendingRevisionStatus.description:
Lifecycle states a pending-revision row can be in.

`#[non_exhaustive]` so a future state (e.g. `Superseded`) is not a
breaking change for downstream matchers.

PendingRevisionView.description:
One row in the reviewer-facing pending list.

`body` is intentionally omitted from the listing shape: the queue is
usually long and the SPA fetches the full row through
[`PendingRevisionDetailResponse`] when the reviewer opens an entry. The
page slug + author label is joined in so the list can render without a
second round trip.

oneOf.description: Authenticated author id, or `None` for anonymous edits.

author_label.description: Username snapshot, joined in for anonymous-friendly display.

comment.description: Operator-visible comment / edit summary attached to the edit.

decided_at.description: When the reviewer acted, or `None` while pending.

namespace_id.description: Namespace the target page lives in (joined in).

page_title.description: Title of the page at queue time (joined in).

oneOf.description: Parent revision id the edit was based on.

rejection_reason.description: Operator-visible note attached to a rejection.

oneOf.description: Reviewer who decided the row, or `None` while pending.

protection_level.description:
New protection level for the page. Accepted wire forms mirror the
snake-case serde representation of [`ProtectionLevel`]:
`"none"`, `"semi_protected"`, `"protected"`, `"fully_protected"`.

ProtectionLevel.description:
How aggressively a page is protected from edits.

The semantics:

- [`None`](ProtectionLevel::None) — anyone with the `EDIT` permission can
  edit. The default for new pages.
- [`SemiProtected`](ProtectionLevel::SemiProtected) — requires an
  established account (no anonymous edits).
- [`Protected`](ProtectionLevel::Protected) — requires an editor-or-higher
  role.
- [`FullyProtected`](ProtectionLevel::FullyProtected) — requires an admin.

The exact mapping from variant to required role is decided in the auth
layer; this enum is purely a data carrier.

RateLimitErrorBody.description: Wire form for a 429 response body.

RecentChangeView.description:
A single entry in the recent-changes feed.

Constructed by the handler from a `thewiki_storage::repo::RecentChange`
— one revision flattened with its page, namespace, and author context.

edit_summary.description: Optional short note describing the edit.

namespace_id.description: Namespace the edited page lives in.

page_slug.description: URL slug of the edited page.

revision_id.description: Identifier of the revision this entry refers to.

RecentChangesResponse.description:
Response from `GET /api/v1/recent-changes`.

Items are ordered newest first. `next_cursor` is `None` once the feed has
been exhausted; otherwise pass it back as `?cursor=…` to fetch the next
page. The cursor encodes a fixed `(created_at, id)` boundary so it stays
stable even when new edits land between calls.

items.description: Rows in this batch, ordered `(created_at DESC, id DESC)`.

next_cursor.description:
Token to fetch the next page, or `None` if the feed has been
exhausted.

captcha_response.description:
CAPTCHA challenge token returned by the rendered widget. Required
when `captcha.apply_to_registration = true` on the server; ignored
otherwise. Empty / missing when the noop provider is wired.

display_name.description:
Optional human-readable display name. Falls back to `username` on
the UI side when absent.

email.description:
Optional email address. Stored normalised to lowercase via
[`EmailAddress`].

password.description: Plain-text password. Hashed via Argon2id before storage.

reason.description:
Operator-visible reason. Stored verbatim and echoed back to the
(authenticated) author through the in-app inbox. Must be non-empty
after trimming — the handler returns 400 if the trimmed value is
empty. The schema-level `minLength: 1` is a coarse pre-validation
hint for OpenAPI consumers.

from_revision.description: Revision to revert *to*. Must belong to the page named in the path.

message.description:
Optional short note describing the revert (think Git commit message).
Falls back to `"Reverted to <revision id>"` when omitted.

RevisionListResponse.description:
Response from `GET /api/v1/pages/{slug}/revisions`.

Items are newest-first per the storage contract. `next_cursor` is `None`
once the history has been fully walked; otherwise pass it back as
`?cursor=…` to fetch the next page.

items.description: Revisions in this batch, newest first.

next_cursor.description:
Token to fetch the next page, or `None` if there are no more
revisions.

RevisionView.description:
Compact summary of a revision used by the list endpoint.

`body_excerpt` is the first ~200 characters of the revision body. It's a
rendered string (not bytes), counted by `chars`, so multi-byte runes are
not split halfway through.

body_excerpt.description:
First ~200 characters of the body. Use the diff endpoint to compare
the full body against another revision.

edit_summary.description: Optional short note describing the edit (think Git commit message).

oneOf.description:
Previous revision in the page's history, or `None` for the first
revision.

SearchHitView.description:
One ranked hit returned by the search endpoint.

Constructed from a [`thewiki_search::SearchHit`] in the handler. The
snippet carries HTML — clients are expected to sanitise it before
rendering (the SPA pipes it through DOMPurify).

namespace_slug.description: Slug of the namespace the hit lives in.

score.description:
BM25-derived relevance score (higher is better). Useful only as a
relative ranking within the same response.

slug.description: URL slug of the matched page.

snippet.description:
HTML snippet of the body with matched terms wrapped in
`<mark>…</mark>`. May be empty when the match only touched the
title — the SPA renders the title alone in that case.

updated_at.description: Last-edited timestamp of the matched page.

SearchResponse.description:
Response body for `GET /api/v1/search`.

`next_cursor` is reserved for relevance-cursor pagination; today every
response carries `null` and `total_estimate` reflects the number of hits
materialised in this response. The shape stays stable so a follow-up
can wire cursors without changing the wire surface.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next batch — currently always `null`.

total_estimate.description: Best-effort estimate of the total matching document count.

SignatureConvention.description:
Sign-with-timestamp convention metadata (#43).

Mirrors the server-side `~~~~` expansion so the SPA can preview the
rendered signature client-side without a round-trip. The marker is
MediaWiki-compatible — operators familiar with `mw:Help:Signatures`
won't have to retrain editors.

format.description:
Format string describing the expansion. `{user}` is replaced with
`[[User:<username>]]`; `{timestamp}` with an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp.

marker.description: Marker that triggers signature expansion. Always `"~~~~"`.

tag.description: The tag value as it would appear on `page_tags.tag`.

UpdateNamespaceRequest.description:
Body of `PATCH /api/v1/namespaces/{slug}`.

Slug renames are deliberately not supported — they invalidate URLs and
have cascading effects on the link graph, search index, and audit log.
Operators who really need to rename a namespace can create a new one,
move pages across via the admin tools (future work), and delete the
empty original.

categories.description:
New category set. When present the entire set is replaced
(so passing `[]` clears every assignment). Omit to leave the
existing set untouched.

content.description: New body. Always required — an update commits a new revision.

edit_summary.description: Optional short note describing the edit (think Git commit message).

tags.description: New tag set. Same replace-on-present semantics as `categories`.

title.description: New title. Omitting it keeps the existing title.

UrlBlocklistView.description: URL blocklist row as returned by the API.

UserPayload.description: JSON body for a successful login / `/me`.

display_name.description: Display name (falls back to username at the UI layer).

permissions.description:
Effective permission flags, pipe-separated (e.g. `"READ | EDIT"`).
Convenience for the SPA so it doesn't need to re-evaluate the role
table.

roles.description:
Role names the user holds. Permission bits are not exposed on the
wire to keep the surface stable across permission-set changes.

WatchStatus.description: Response from `POST /api/v1/watchlist` and the toggle path on the SPA.

watched.description:
Whether the page is currently on the caller's watchlist after the
operation.

WatchedPageView.description:
A single row on the user's watchlist.

Constructed from a `thewiki_storage::repo::WatchedPage` — the row already
joins in namespace slug and page title so the SPA can render the list
without follow-up lookups.

namespace.description: Namespace slug the page lives in.

slug.description: URL slug of the page within its namespace.

watched_at.description: When the user added the page to their watchlist.

items.description: Pages on the caller's watchlist, newest first.

CsrfToken.name: x-csrf-token
CsrfToken.description: Double-submit CSRF token matching the thewiki_csrf cookie.

SessionCookie.name: thewiki_session
SessionCookie.description: Opaque session cookie issued by POST /api/v1/auth/login.

tags.name: pages
tags.description: Page CRUD (legacy `/api/v1/pages/...`, defaults to the `Main` namespace; clients should migrate to `wiki` per #28)
